cards.title: Repo Baseline
cards.summary: Minimum operating contract for any non-trivial task in this repo.
use_when: starting or resuming a task
you need the repo contract before implementation
you want the minimum safe rules without full docs
rules: Prefer declarative Nix/module changes before runtime or script fallbacks.
Collect small, high-signal evidence before editing or rebuilding.
Every change needs validation evidence and a rollback note.
Use repo structure lint before accepting file layout changes.
Do not hardcode secrets, ports, or service URLs.
Avoid repeated rebuilds until the failing layer is classified.
Keep onboarding shallow first; expand only if the task proves it necessary.
anti_patterns: loading large policy docs before the first concrete task signal
editing code before classifying the failure layer
hardcoding ports or URLs into modules or scripts

cards.title: Task Execution
cards.summary: Compact execution loop for making changes without losing evidence or wasting context.
use_when: moving from discovery into edits
splitting a task into verifiable slices
you need a fast evidence contract
rules: Define objective, constraints, out-of-scope, and acceptance checks first.
Group work into one logical change per validation cycle.
Before edits, identify the smallest command that can falsify the current assumption.
Record files changed, commands run, tests run, evidence, and rollback for each slice.
Reject slices that have no validation evidence.
Prefer small local verification before heavier integration checks.
anti_patterns: big-bang patches without intermediate evidence
running full rebuilds before cheaper package-level checks
accepting planner or runner changes without fixture coverage

cards.title: Runtime Incident
cards.summary: Low-token diagnosis loop for service, linkage, confinement, and runtime-probe failures.
use_when: a systemd service is unhealthy or slow
package linkage and runtime behavior disagree
confinement or AppArmor may be involved
rules: Run the phase runner before opening full logs.
Classify the failure layer before patching or rebuilding.
Compare original binary path vs copied alias path early when confinement is plausible.
Separate package linkage failures from runtime-probe failures.
Open deeper logs only for the failing layer.
Prefer planner and runner outputs over ad hoc command churn.
Stop once runtime acceleration or expected probe success is confirmed.
anti_patterns: blind rebuild loops
assuming a driver problem before checking confinement and linkage
patching upstream code before proving the failing layer

cards.title: Nix Module Change
cards.summary: Compact guidance for changes that should land declaratively and survive rebuilds safely.
use_when: changing service wiring, options, ports, or packages declaratively
touching Nix modules or flake outputs
you need rollback-safe deployment behavior
rules: Prefer typed options and env injection over hardcoded literals.
Use one owner per option; avoid conflicting defaults.
Make hardware-aware or role-aware choices explicit in module logic.
Validate evaluation or build paths before switching live systems.
Keep rollback commands ready before activation.
anti_patterns: hardcoded ports or service URLs in scripts or modules
runtime-only fixes when a declarative module change is viable
switching live systems before package-level or toplevel build passes

cards.title: System-Level Fix
cards.summary: Guidance for NixOS/system-level debugging, evaluation, and rollback-safe fixes.
use_when: fixing host-level NixOS or service wiring issues
working on rebuild, switch, boot, or rollback safety
changing system-level behavior rather than app-only behavior
rules: Classify whether the failure is evaluation, build, activation, or runtime before editing broadly.
Prefer package-level or toplevel build validation before live switching.
Keep rollback commands ready before any activation step.
Distinguish host-specific facts from reusable module logic.
Use declarative Nix fixes before imperative service mutations.
anti_patterns: switching live systems before cheaper validation steps pass
mixing host facts and reusable module logic in one patch without clear boundaries
using imperative hotfixes where a declarative module change is viable

cards.title: Feature Development
cards.summary: Progressive-disclosure guidance for new feature implementation on a mature codebase.
use_when: building a new capability instead of only fixing a regression
working in existing code that needs scoped planning
you need a minimal planning surface before implementation
rules: Start with a primer or brownfield plan before loading broad architecture docs.
Slice implementation into one logical change per validation cycle.
Prefer the smallest repo-grounded context that explains current behavior.
Promote repeated ad hoc steps into tested tooling only after the workflow proves stable.
Keep acceptance checks concrete and measurable.
commands: scripts/ai/aqd workflows brownfield --target . --objective "plan next validated slice"
anti_patterns: loading full policy and architecture docs before the first repo-grounded plan
combining discovery, implementation, and acceptance into one large unverified patch
inventing new workflows when aqd primer or brownfield already covers the need

cards.title: PRSI Operations
cards.summary: Bounded context for pessimistic recursive self-improvement loops, gates, and artifacts.
use_when: working on PRSI orchestration, gates, or artifacts
changing the self-improvement control plane
validating PRSI policy, budget, or quarantine logic
rules: Keep PRSI changes pessimistic: gated, bounded, and artifact-backed.
Prefer policy/config/schema changes over hidden behavior in code.
Validate budget, approval, and quarantine behavior before widening automation.
Treat PRSI runbooks and schemas as part of the contract, not optional docs.
Use dry-run evidence and static gates before live execution.
anti_patterns: treating PRSI as a normal feature without gate or artifact validation
expanding automation before budget and approval rules are validated
changing orchestrator behavior without corresponding runbook/schema/test updates

cards.title: Harness-First Workflow
cards.summary: Compact context for tasks that should start with planner/hints/workflow surfaces instead of direct ad hoc execution.
use_when: the task is complex enough to benefit from hints and workflow selection
you want the minimal harness-first contract
you need a low-token way to decide the next tool path
rules: Use hints and workflow primers before broad manual exploration on non-trivial tasks.
Prefer one small workflow output over many exploratory shell calls.
Defer loading deeper docs until the workflow output leaves a specific gap.
Keep evidence and rollback attached to each execution slice.
commands: scripts/ai/aqd workflows brownfield --target . --objective "plan next validated slice"
anti_patterns: starting complex work with broad manual exploration and no workflow output
loading full runbooks before asking hints or primer tools for the next step
treating harness-first as optional for multi-layer tasks

cards.title: Capability Gap
cards.summary: Guidance for missing tools, workflows, skills, and command-not-found failures.
use_when: a requested tool is not installed or not on PATH
a workflow or skill reference is missing
you need a declarative remediation path for missing capabilities
rules: Classify the capability gap before installing or patching anything.
Prefer declarative Nix or repo-local fixes over imperative one-off installs.
Treat workflow blueprints and skill bodies as first-class capabilities.
Catalog repeated missing-capability fixes so future agents do not rediscover them.

cards.title: Token Discipline
cards.summary: Rules for keeping context cost low without starving the task of needed signal.
use_when: you are tuning onboarding or agent behavior
the task risks context bloat
you want a shallow-to-deep disclosure path
rules: Start with one brief context card plus one task-specific hint query.
Escalate from brief to standard only when the first pass leaves a concrete gap.
Escalate to deep only for the failing layer, not the whole system.
Prefer structured outputs and classifications over long prose logs.
Do not load full docs when a small command or card can answer the next question.
Add tests around new automation so future agents need less explanatory context.
Treat cloud-hosted or OpenRouter-routed models as a pressure valve for RAM and context, not as permission for unbounded token spend.
Use a lean first pass, then increase token budget only when architecture depth, broad comparison, or repeated failures justify it.
Keep reusable prompt prefixes stable and compact so provider-side prompt caching can reduce repeated token cost.
anti_patterns: front-loading whole runbooks into context before task classification
using deep disclosure as the default
adding tooling without regression coverage or a short operator contract
sending every task to a remote or free-tier model when a smaller local or retrieval-first pass would answer it
treating provider flexibility as a substitute for budget guardrails

cards.title: Context Offload
cards.summary: Harness-native pattern for long-running work that keeps remote prompts lean by storing, recalling, and compacting context locally.
use_when: the task will span many turns or phases
remote context windows or token spend are becoming the bottleneck
you want the harness to serve prior context on demand instead of resending whole histories
rules: Prefer harness session state, memory recall, and context cards before resending old prompt history.
Compact often: preserve decisions, blockers, evidence, and next actions, not full conversational prose.
Store durable decisions and discoveries in shared or agent memory so later turns can retrieve them directly.
Use progressive disclosure: brief card first, then recall only the active layer from local retrieval.
Re-measure latency and recall quality after widening retrieval breadth; do not assume more context is better.
Keep remote prompts stable and compact so provider-side caching can work across turns.
anti_patterns: resending full conversation history to a remote model every turn
treating compaction as an end-of-task activity instead of a running control loop
broad route_search before checking whether harness memory already holds the needed decision or discovery
